commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh A man, having separated himself, seeks his own desire, and rages against all sound wisdom. True friendship resembles charity, and bears all things, 1 Corinthians xiii. Hebrew now reads Thave, "desire "instead of Thuane, occasion, or "pretext "which must have been in the copies of the Septuagint and Vulgate. (Calmet) Protestants, "through desire, a man having separated himself, seeketh and intermeddleth with all wisdom. "The solitary seeks heaven.
